description: Blind mechanistic archetype-adjacency graph, built from vocab glosses only (no benchmark access).

neighbors.rationale: Both obtain a unit's behavior from a self-consistent closure over the whole population.
Both solve a fixed-point where the produced aggregate equals the imposed input.
The self-consistent aggregate turning nonzero selects one of several equivalent states.
The population-averaged aggregate is the order parameter rising past a critical point.
Single-unit marginal closed self-consistently across the population is mean-field.
Both pass local marginals treating neighbors as independent, exact on trees.
Neighbors treated as independent and the description closed self-consistently.
Fixed point where imposed aggregate equals produced aggregate is mean-field self-consistency.
A self-consistent closure whose operating point is a fixed point.
A quantity that enters the equation determining itself is a self-reinforcing loop.
Output fed back to set the operating point is a regulated feedback loop.
Frozen (quenched) disorder produces the competing, unsatisfiable constraints.
Quenched randomness underlies glassy trapping and aging.
Frozen disorder generates a rugged multi-minimum landscape.
Both treat frozen random couplings as fixed disorder to average over.
An order parameter growing from zero past threshold breaks symmetry continuously.
A symmetric state birthing a symmetric pair is the bifurcation form of symmetry breaking.
The self-consistent aggregate selects one of several equivalent configurations.
Defects are the singularities left in a broken-symmetry order field.
Continuous onset of an order parameter breaks a symmetry.
The aggregate observable is a self-consistent order parameter.
Diverging susceptibility and correlation length near the transition slow relaxation.
Second-order transitions are classified by universal critical exponents.
Observables near the critical point collapse under critical rescaling.
Diverging correlation length makes the critical point scale-invariant.
Contrasting decay of a metastable/unstable state, barrier vs barrierless.
Nucleated domains grow and demix the system.
Discontinuous jump with path-dependent hysteresis between metastable states.
A discontinuous, hysteretic jump to an alternative state.
Decay proceeds by escape over a barrier via a critical nucleus.
Contrasting mechanisms for decay of a metastable homogeneous state.
Barrierless decomposition demixes into growing domains.
A homogeneous state going linearly unstable to fluctuations across length scales.
Defects are singularities in the broken-symmetry order field.
Defects and domain walls anneal as domains coarsen.
Differential spreading of short-range activator and long-range inhibitor is the Turing mechanism.
A homogeneous state destabilized linearly by infinitesimal fluctuations.
Reaction-diffusion machinery producing stationary spatial structure.
Short-range self-amplification vs long-range suppression is the Turing pattern mechanism.
The same competing ranges sustain localized spots and pulses.
Activator-inhibitor kinetics propagate fronts and waves.
Excitable media propagate stereotyped self-sustaining pulses.
Self-sustaining propagating pulses maintained far from equilibrium.
Reaction-diffusion kinetics drive the moving interface.
Spinodal decomposition initiates the demixing that then coarsens.
Nucleated domains grow and coarsen over time.
Both are domain/interface growth with power-law scaling in time.
Copying dynamics coarsen into growing like-state domains.
Competing activation/inhibition ranges sustain the localized structures.
Reaction-diffusion patterning far from equilibrium.
Bounded pulses maintained by continuous throughput of energy/matter.
Interface/domain growth with power laws in elapsed time.
Roughening exponents are universal, independent of microscopic detail.

Width curves collapse under the scaling exponents.
Continuum limit describing the same evolution of a probability distribution.
Its stationary state with balanced microscopic transition fluxes.
Occupancy transfers among discrete states at prescribed rates.
Birth-death transitions are a canonical discrete jump process.
The distribution-level dual of the same drift-plus-noise dynamics.
Continuum limit of the discrete-state balance equation.
Stochastic spreading is diffusion of the density.

The zero-current stationary state of the density.
The SDE whose density obeys the drift-diffusion equation.
Explicit random forcing drives the noise-activated escape.

Random forcing integrates into diffusive displacement.
Its stationary state with each transition balanced by its reverse.

The zero-current equilibrium of the density.
